all.en: Last Sunday, I finally sat down at the kitchen table to plan my monthly budget.
all.zh: 上週日，我終於坐在餐桌前規劃我的月度預算。

all.en: My older sister had warned me that adult life is full of unexpected expenses.
all.zh: 我姊姊曾警告我，成年人的生活充滿了意想不到的開銷。

all.en: I opened a spreadsheet and listed my salary at the top in bold letters.
all.zh: 我打開試算表，把薪水以粗體字列在最上方。

all.en: Then I wrote down all the fixed costs, like rent, electricity, and my phone bill.
all.zh: 接著我寫下所有的固定支出，例如房租、電費和手機費。

all.en: I was shocked to discover how much I had been spending on coffee every week.
all.zh: 我很驚訝地發現自己每週在咖啡上花了多少錢。

all.en: I decided to set aside thirty percent of my income for savings each month.
all.zh: 我決定每月把收入的百分之三十存起來。

all.en: Another twenty percent would go toward food, transportation, and small daily needs.
all.zh: 另外百分之二十會用在食物、交通和小額日常需求上。

all.en: The rest would cover entertainment, gifts for friends, and the occasional impulse buy.
all.zh: 剩下的則用於娛樂、送朋友禮物和偶爾的衝動購物。

all.en: At first, sticking to the plan felt like a strict diet for my wallet.
all.zh: 一開始，遵守這個計畫就像給我的錢包嚴格節食一樣。

all.en: By the end of the first month, I had saved more than I ever imagined.
all.zh: 到第一個月底，我存下的金額比我想像的還要多。

all.en: Budgeting taught me that small daily choices add up to big financial results.
all.zh: 預算管理教會我，每天的小選擇會累積成巨大的財務成果。
